narrative.summary:
📍 SoZo Sip Bar + Omakase
319 Bowery, New York, NY 10003, USA

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on the Bowery, where the Whole Foods crowd meets old-guard graffiti artists, and SoZo sits in that in-between with clean lines and blonde wood. The counter seats maybe ten—close enough to watch the chef's hands work but not so tight you're in your neighbor's conversation. Low ambient light catches the ceramic plates and sake bottles lined up behind the bar like a ceramics collection that actually gets used.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Each piece of nigiri gets just enough—a brush of nikiri, a whisper of yuzu, maybe charred scallion—so you taste the fish first and the flourish second. The fish itself is pristine, sliced with precision, though you might wish for slightly thicker cuts if you're used to West Coast portions. Expect to spend around $150-200 for the full omakase, more if you're working through their sake list, which you should.

blocks.title: Sozo Sip Bar + Omakase
blocks.body:
📍 SoZo Sip Bar + Omakase
319 Bowery, New York, NY 10003, USA

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Affordable 15-course omakase for $89 on the Bowery. Smoked salmon sushi, uni temaki, toro hand rolls, plus mochi. Speakeasy sip bar with rare sakes and Japanese whisky. Just opened March 2026. 5.0 rating. Great value for quality omakase.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The chef curated each course with such care — every piece was perfectly balanced, fresh, and beautifully presented."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Excellent service, fantastic sake, clean and fresh fish with unique combinations of flavor. Truly excellent. Could have larger slices of fish, but doesn’t leave you needing more food."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "In a city flooded with new omakase openings, it’s rare to find one that feels this dialed-in from day one.

The food is outstanding— exceptionally fresh and beautifully executed. Each piece is delicately layered with flavor—just enough to enhance the fish, not mask it. The warm and polished service elevates the dining experience. The space is chic and sophisticated, with a NYC-meets-Tokyo aesthetic. Perfect for a date night.

It's an incredible value for omakase (I’ve tried more than my fair share across all price points.) I don't write reviews often, but I had to share this standout debut. I’m already looking forward to returning."
